PkgSurfaceSweep2Ref.name: PkgSurfaceSweep2Ref
PkgSurfaceSweep2Ref.detailed: Baruch Zukerman, Ron Wein, and Efi Fogel This package provides three free functions implemented based on the sweep-line paradigm: given a collection of input curves, compute all intersection points, compute the set of subcurves that are pairwise interior-disjoint induced by them, and check whether there is at least one pair of curves among them that intersect in their interior. Introduced in: CGAL 2.4 Depends on: PkgArrangementOnSurface2 BibTeX: cgal:wfz-ic2-26a License: GPL This chapter describes three functions implemented using the sweep-line algorithm: given a collection $ {\mathcal C}$ of planar curves, compute all intersection points among them, obtain the set of maximal pairwise interior-disjoint subcurves of the curves in $ {\mathcal C}$ , or check whether there is at least one pair of curves in $ {\mathcal C}$ that intersect. By default the curves are considered closed. The first two operations are performed in an output-sensitive manner. Classified Reference Pages Functions CGAL::compute_intersection_points CGAL::compute_subcurves CGAL::do_intersect

members.name: compute_intersection_points
members.brief: Given a range of curves, compute all intersection points between two (or more) input curves.
members.detailed: When the flag report_endpoints is true , this function reports all the curve endpoints as well. If a curve endpoint is also an intersection point, it is reported once (regardless of the value of the report_endpoints flag). The value-type of InputIterator is a curve type and the value-type of OutputIterator is a point type. The output points are reported in an increasing $xy$ -lexicographical order.
members.argsstring: (InputIterator curves_begin, InputIterator curves_end, OutputIterator points, bool report_endpoints=false)

members.name: compute_intersection_points
members.brief: Given a range of curves, compute all intersection points between two (or more) input curves.
members.detailed: When the flag report_endpoints is true , this function reports all the curve endpoints as well. If a curve endpoint is also an intersection point, it is reported once (regardless of the value of the report_endpoints flag). The Traits type must be a model of the AosTraits_2 concept, such that the value-type of InputIterator is Traits::Curve_2 , and the value-type of OutputIterator is Traits::Point_2 . The output points are reported in an increasing $xy$ -lexicographical order.
members.argsstring: (InputIterator curves_begin, InputIterator curves_end, OutputIterator points, bool report_endpoints=false, Traits traits)

members.name: compute_subcurves
members.brief: Given a range of curves, compute all $ x$ -monotone subcurves that are pairwise disjoint in their interior, as induced by the input curves.
members.detailed: If the flag multiple_overlaps is true , then a subcurve that represents an overlap of $ k$ input curves is reported $ k$ times; otherwise, each subcurve is reported only once. The value-type of InputIterator is a curve type, and the value-type of OutputIterator is an $x$ -monotone curve type.
members.argsstring: (InputIterator curves_begin, InputIterator curves_end, OutputIterator subcurves, bool multiple_overlaps=false)

members.name: compute_subcurves
members.brief: Given a range of curves, compute all $ x$ -monotone subcurves that are pairwise disjoint in their interior, as induced by the input curves.
members.detailed: If the flag multiple_overlaps is true , then a subcurve that represents an overlap of $ k$ input curves is reported $ k$ times; otherwise, each subcurve is reported only once. The Traits type must be a model of the AosTraits_2 concept, such that the value-type of InputIterator is Traits::Curve_2 , and the value-type of OutputIterator is Traits::X_monotone_curve_2 .
members.argsstring: (InputIterator curves_begin, InputIterator curves_end, OutputIterator subcurves, bool multiple_overlaps=false, Traits traits=Default_traits())

members.name: do_curves_intersect
members.detailed: Deprecated Call Surface_sweep_2::do_intersect(curves_begin, curves_end, false) instead Given a range of curves, check whether there is at least one pair of curves that intersect in their interior. The function returns true if such a pair is found, and false if all curves are pairwise disjoint in their interior. The value-type of InputIterator is a curve type. The call CGAL::do_curves_intersect(begin, end, traits) should be replaced by the call CGAL::Surface_sweep_2::do_intersect(begin, end, false, traits)

members.name: do_curves_intersect
members.detailed: Deprecated Call Surface_sweep_2::do_intersect(curves_begin, curves_end, false, traits) instead Given a range of curves, check whether there is at least one pair of curves that intersect in their interior. The function returns true if such a pair is found, and false if all curves are pairwise disjoint in their interior. The Traits type must be a model of the AosTraits_2 concept, such that the value-type of InputIterator is Traits::Curve_2 . The call CGAL::do_curves_intersect(begin, end) should be replaced by the call CGAL::Surface_sweep_2::do_intersect(begin, end, false)

members.name: do_intersect
members.brief: Given a range of curves, check whether there is at least one pair of curves that intersect.
members.detailed: The value-type of InputIterator is a curve type. If consider_common_endpoints is true , common endpoints are considered. Otherwise, common endpoints are not counted as intersections.

members.name: do_intersect
members.brief: Given a range of curves, check whether there is at least one pair of curves that intersect.
members.detailed: The value-type of InputIterator is a curve type. If consider_common_endpoints is true , common endpoints are considered. Otherwise, common endpoints are not counted as intersections. The Traits type must be a model of the AosTraits_2 concept, such that the value-type of InputIterator is either Traits::Curve_2 or Traits::X_monotone_curve_2 .
members.argsstring: (InputIterator curves_begin, InputIterator curves_end, bool consider_common_endpoints=true, Traits traits=Default_traits())
